title: Massachusetts law about lead poisoning and control
content:
Massachusetts law about lead poisoning and control

Laws, regulations, cases and web sources on lead paint law.

Key Massachusetts Laws:
- MGL c. 62, s. 6(e) - Tax credits for lead abatement
- MGL c. 93A - Regulation of business practices for consumer protection (s.2 Unfair practices, s.9 Civil actions and remedies, s.11 Actions for unfair trade practices)
- MGL c. 111, s. 127B-1/2 - Petition for agreement to remove lead paint; costs; authority to evict
- MGL c. 111, ss. 189A-199B - Lead poisoning prevention and control
- MGL c. 112, s. 12BB - Lead poisoning screening (mandatory screenings for children under age 6)
- MGL c. 151B, s. 4(11) - Unlawful practices: Discrimination against families with young children with respect to residential properties containing dangerous levels of lead
- MGL c. 175, s. 111H - Liability insurance for residential property owners complying with lead poisoning prevention guidelines
- MGL c. 186, s. 18 - Reprisal for reporting violations of law or for tenant's union activity

Key Massachusetts Regulations:
- 105 CMR 410 - Minimum standards of fitness for human habitation (State sanitary code, chapter II); 410.502 Use of lead paint prohibited in dwellings
- 105 CMR 460 - Lead poisoning prevention and control (mandatory screening for children under 6, lead abatement, training and registration of lead inspectors, enforcement)
- 454 CMR 22 - Deleading and lead-safe renovation regulations
- 454 CMR 23 - Occupational lead poisoning registry

Key Cases:
- Bencosme v. Kokoras, 400 Mass. 40 (1987): Owners of residential property are strictly liable for injuries sustained under the lead paint act.
- Navarro v. Burgess, 99 Mass. App. Ct. 466 (2021): Lead inspector owed no duty to person who became a tenant more than 20 years after inspection.

Forms:
- Property transfer lead paint notification & certification form
- Short term vacation or recreational rental exemption form
- Tenant lead law notification form (available in English and Spanish)
